batch_summary: Deed [306] — Warranty deed, W. E. Black (widower) and children → J. T. Black, 56.8 ac Lipscomb Norvell headright, Limestone County. Executed Nov 27, 1942; filed/recorded Jan 2, 1943. $1,000 cash; vendor's lien retained; surface + minerals, no reservation. ISSUE-43 item 4 PULLED. Critical finding: this deed covers ONLY the Norvell Survey — the 'Boyd chain' label on item 4 was incorrect. JT Black's Boyd Survey acquisition source remains unidentified. W. E. Black's full family confirmed: children Hallie (m. Milburn), Ella (m. Rowell), C. E. Black (confirmed sibling, not parent), Zona (m. Thompson), J. A. Black, Leta Black, plus W. D. Swint and children. Ida A. Black confirmed deceased by Nov 1942. DISPOSED PROPERTY — no bearing on current holdings; Norvell tract left family chain in 1965 via deed [316].

01_FAMILY_ENTITY_STRUCTURE.md.timestamp_summary: Deed [306] PDF pulled — W. E. Black family confirmed (8+ children identified); C.E. Black confirmed sibling of JT Black; Ida A. Black deceased by Nov 1942; Norvell Survey only (NOT Boyd); ISSUE-43 item 4 satisfied

operations.find: C.E. Black was likely first-named grantor among W.E. Black's children in the item 4 deed. ⚠️ The [314] affidavit references the Norvell Survey, while item 4 is labeled "Boyd chain" — item 4 may be a separate deed or a multi-survey instrument covering both Boyd and Norvell tracts. Pull still needed to confirm.
operations.replace_with: C.E. Black was alphabetically first among W.E. Black's children in the deed (explaining the LC index entry "BLACK C E +"). **Deed [306] (PDF CONFIRMED 2026-04-01)**: Warranty deed, W. E. Black (widower) and children → J. T. Black (Navarro County); 56.8 ac Lipscomb Norvell headright ONLY — **NOT Boyd Survey**. The ISSUE-43 item 4 "Boyd chain" label was incorrect; this deed covers only the Norvell tract. Full grantor group: W. E. Black, Hallie Milburn (& J.R. Milburn), Ella Rowell (& G.L. Rowell), C. E. Black (& Erna Black), Zona Thompson (& F.P. Thompson), J. A. Black, Leta Black; plus W. D. Swint and children (J.W. Swint & Bertha, A.C. Swint & Jestena, Essie Campbell & I.L. Campbell). W.D. Swint's surname differs from Black — relationship unclear (likely married a deceased Black daughter or related through Ida A. Black). $1,000 cash; vendor's lien retained. No mineral reservation. Acknowledgments: Limestone Co. (Nov 27, 1942, E.C. Trotter NP), Delta Co. (C.E. Black+Erna Dec 4; Thompsons Dec 9, 1942; L.B. Taylor NP), Maricopa Co. AZ (J.A. Black Dec 22, 1942, G.P. Furr NP), Dallas Co. (Leta Black Dec 14, 1942, W.D. Bates NP). George Rowell and Joe Milburn signed with marks (x). Filed Jan 2, 1943 8 AM; recorded Jan 2, 1943 2 PM; Vell W. Basden Deputy; John Kidd County Clerk LC. Revenue stamps $1.10. Metes-and-bounds identical to [314]/[316]. JT Black's Boyd Survey acquisition source remains unidentified — a separate deed must exist. ISSUE-43 item 4 PULLED. DISPOSED — property left family chain via [316] (1965).

03_DEED_CHAIN_SUMMARY.md.timestamp_summary: Deed [306] PDF pulled — W. E. Black et al. → J. T. Black warranty deed (1942, Norvell Survey 56.8 ac); ISSUE-43 item 4 satisfied; NOT Boyd chain

operations.description: Add deed [306] entry to Phase 2, before entry 2 ([312]) — chronologically earliest JT Black chain instrument
operations.anchor: 2. `1959-02-09_[312]_l` — Kelly Bounds + Jeff Bounds (affiants) → Public (affidavit of heirship, Limestone)
operations.content: 1b. `1942-11-27_[306]_l` — W. E. Black (widower) and children: Hallie Milburn (& J.R. Milburn), Ella Rowell (& G.L. Rowell), C. E. Black (& Erna Black), Zona Thompson (& F.P. Thompson), J. A. Black, Leta Black; plus W. D. Swint and children: J.W. Swint (& Bertha), A.C. Swint (& Jestena), Essie Campbell (& I.L. Campbell) → J. T. Black (warranty deed, surface+minerals, Limestone) — 56.8 ac, part of Lipscomb Norvell headright, Limestone County. Beginning at T.W. Wade's SW corner in south line of 400-ac John Caruthers survey from Lipscomb Norvell; S 430 vrs to stake in N line of Jones 150-ac survey; E 745 vrs to Jones NE corner; N 430 vrs to stake in S line of Caruthers 400-ac survey; W 745-8/10 vrs to POB. $1,000 cash; vendor's lien retained ("when this deed shall become absolute"). No mineral reservation — surface + minerals conveyed in full warranty. W. E. Black acknowledged as widower (Ida A. Black deceased by Nov 1942). JT Black grantee residence: Navarro County, Texas. Revenue stamps $1.10 (IRS). Multiple acknowledgment locations: Limestone Co. (Nov 27, 1942, E.C. Trotter NP — W.E. Black, Milburns, Rowells, W.D. Swint, J.W. Swints, A.C. Swints, Campbells), Delta Co. (Dec 4, C.E. Black+Erna; Dec 9, Thompsons — L.B. Taylor NP), Maricopa Co. AZ (Dec 22, J.A. Black — G.P. Furr NP, comm. exp. Jun 28, 1946), Dallas Co. (Dec 14, Leta Black — W.D. Bates NP). George Rowell and Joe Milburn signed with marks (x). LC Vol. 278, Pg. 379–382, Inst# 1943000000000013. Filed Jan 2, 1943 at 8 AM; recorded Jan 2, 1943 at 2 PM; John Kidd, County Clerk; Vell W. Basden, Deputy. **DISPOSED PROPERTY** — metes-and-bounds identical to [314]/[316]. Norvell Survey disposition chain: W.E. Black + Ida A. Black (pre-~1925) → JT Black ([306], 1943) → heirs by intestacy → Verona E. Black ([311], 1959) → Mary Rees Wilson ([316], 1965). **⚠️ NOT Boyd Survey**: ISSUE-43 item 4 was labeled "Boyd chain" but this deed covers only the Norvell tract. JT Black's Boyd Survey acquisition source remains unidentified. ISSUE-43 item 4.

operations.description: Add deed [306] analysis entry before the [314] analysis entry
operations.content: - **Deed [306] analysis (2026-04-01)**: Warranty deed (LC Vol. 278, Pg. 379–382, Inst# 1943000000000013, executed 11/27/1942, filed 1/2/1943, recorded 1/2/1943). Grantors: W. E. Black (widower) and children — Hallie Milburn (& J.R. Milburn), Ella Rowell (& G.L. Rowell), C. E. Black (& Erna Black), Zona Thompson (& F.P. Thompson), J. A. Black, Leta Black; plus W. D. Swint and children (J.W. Swint & Bertha, A.C. Swint & Jestena, Essie Campbell & I.L. Campbell). Grantee: J. T. Black (Navarro County, Texas). Property: 56.8 ac part of Lipscomb Norvell headright, Limestone County. Metes-and-bounds identical to [314] affidavit and [316] Wilson deed. Consideration: $1,000 cash; vendor's lien retained. Surface + minerals — no mineral reservation. **Key findings**: (a) **NOT Boyd Survey** — ISSUE-43 item 4 was labeled "Boyd chain" but deed [306] covers only the Lipscomb Norvell headright. JT Black's Boyd Survey acquisition source must be a separate, unlocated instrument. This resolves the [314] analysis question about whether item 4 was a multi-survey instrument. (b) **W. E. Black family confirmed**: W.E. Black is JT Black's father, signing as "a widower" (Ida A. Black deceased by Nov 1942). Children identified: Hallie (m. Milburn), Ella (m. Rowell), C.E. Black, Zona (m. Thompson), J.A. Black, Leta Black. The W.D. Swint lineage (different surname) likely connects through a deceased Black daughter or through Ida A. Black. (c) **C.E. Black confirmed sibling** of JT Black, not parent — reclassification from "probable" to confirmed. C.E. Black acknowledged in Delta County (Dec 4, 1942, L.B. Taylor NP); he was NOT first-named grantor (W.E. Black is the primary grantor), but was alphabetically first among children, explaining the LC index entry "BLACK C E +". (d) **JT Black residence**: Navarro County, Texas in 1942 — not Limestone County. He moved to Limestone County before his death (1959). (e) **Acknowledgment span**: Nov 27–Dec 22, 1942 across four counties and two states (TX and AZ) — the family was geographically dispersed. (f) George Rowell and Joe Milburn (= J.R. Milburn) signed with marks (x). (g) Revenue stamps $1.10 (IRS). **DISPOSED PROPERTY** — Norvell Survey disposition chain fully documented: W.E.+Ida Black → JT Black ([306], 1943) → heirs → Verona ([311], 1959) → Wilson ([316], 1965). No bearing on current project holdings. ISSUE-43 item 4 is now PULLED.
